full_response:
We start from the usual (affine) geodesic equation written with the proper time τ :   

(1)  d²x^β/dτ² + Γ^β_{μν}\, (dx^μ/dτ)(dx^ν/dτ) = 0 .

Let σ be any monotonic parameter along the same world-line, x^α = x^α(σ).  
Define the abbreviations  

  ′ ≡ d/dσ ,   ẋ ≡ d/dτ .

Step 1 – Relate the first derivatives  

  ẋ^β = dx^β/dτ = (dx^β/dσ)(dσ/dτ) = x'^β (dσ/dτ).   (2)

Step 2 – Relate the second derivatives  
Using d/dτ = (dσ/dτ) d/dσ we get

  d²x^β/dτ² = d/dτ (ẋ^β)
      = (dσ/dτ) d/dσ [ x'^β (dσ/dτ) ]   
      = (dσ/dτ) [ x''^β (dσ/dτ) + x'^β d/dσ(dσ/dτ) ]  
      = (dσ/dτ)² x''^β + (dσ/dτ) x'^β d/dσ(dσ/dτ).  (3)

Step 3 – Transform the Γ–term  

  Γ^β_{μν} ẋ^μ ẋ^ν  
   = Γ^β_{μν} x'^μ x'^ν (dσ/dτ)².           (4)

Step 4 – Insert (3) and (4) into (1)

0 = (dσ/dτ)² x''^β + (dσ/dτ) x'^β d/dσ(dσ/dτ)
    + Γ^β_{μν} x'^μ x'^ν (dσ/dτ)².                       (5)

Step 5 – Divide by the strictly positive factor (dσ/dτ)² :

0 = x''^β + Γ^β_{μν} x'^μ x'^ν
    + (dτ/dσ) x'^β d/dσ(dσ/dτ).                          (6)

Step 6 – Re-express the last term in logarithmic form.  
Let f(σ) ≡ dτ/dσ  ⇒  dσ/dτ = 1/f.  Then  

(dτ/dσ) d/dσ(dσ/dτ) = f · d/dσ(1/f)
                     = f (-1/f²) df/dσ
                     = –(1/f) df/dσ
                     = – d/dσ[ln f].                     (7)

Hence the bracket in (6) is –d/dσ[ln(dτ/dσ)] x'^β.  
Moving it to the right–hand side finally gives

x''^β + Γ^β_{μν} x'^μ x'^ν
      = d/dσ[ln(dτ/dσ)] x'^β.                           (8)

Equation (8) is precisely the geodesic equation when the world-line is parameterised by an arbitrary variable σ.

\boxed{\displaystyle
\frac{d^{2}x^{\beta}}{d\sigma^{2}}
\;+\;
\Gamma^{\beta}_{\mu\nu}\,
\frac{dx^{\mu}}{d\sigma}\frac{dx^{\nu}}{d\sigma}
\;=\;
\frac{d}{d\sigma}\!\Bigl[\ln\!\Bigl(\frac{d\tau}{d\sigma}\Bigr)\Bigr]\,
\frac{dx^{\beta}}{d\sigma}}
 
Confidence: 86%
